meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to implement testing automation to mitigate the risks of having no version control. Master Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a house of cards. Testing automation acts as your safety net, validating every change before it breaks your project. By integrating Antigravity for test generation and Claude Code for execution, you create a system that catches regressions instantly, ensuring your progress remains stable and deployable.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you just refactored a critical function, and suddenly, the entire application crashes. Without version control, you are left staring at a screen of errors, unable to undo the damage. It is a rite of passage for every developer, but it is one that stops your momentum dead in its tracks.

But what if your code could verify itself? By leveraging modern AI agents to automate testing, you turn your project into a self-validating machine. You don't need a complex Git history to ship with confidence; you need a robust suite of automated checks that ensure your logic holds up, regardless of your workflow.

steps.title: Define Your Critical Path
steps.description: Identify the core function of your app that, if broken, ruins the user experience. We will target this for our first automated test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and identify the single most critical function that requires a unit test to prevent regression.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Identify the most critical function in this project for testing'

steps.title: Generate Test Scenarios
steps.description: Use AI to write comprehensive test cases that cover edge cases you haven't considered yet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of unit tests for the identified critical function, including success paths and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Write a pytest file for the target function with 3 edge cases'

steps.title: Automate the Test Execution
steps.description: Create a script that triggers these tests automatically, ensuring you never deploy broken code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a bash script that executes the newly created test suite and exits with a non-zero code if any test fails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Create a test_runner.sh file and make it executable'

steps.title: Integrate Pre-Run Hooks
steps.description: Ensure your test suite runs before any major changes are finalized in your local development environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: How can I configure my local environment to run my test suite automatically before I save major changes?
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Configure a watch command to run the test suite on file save'

steps.title: Validate Integrity
steps.description: Perform a 'chaos test' by intentionally breaking your code to see if your automated tests catch the error.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a quick sanity check to verify the test suite correctly identifies a deliberate logic error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Run the test suite and confirm it fails on intentional errors'

pro_tips: Always write your tests before the actual code to enforce a Test-Driven Development (TDD) mindset.
Use descriptive naming for your tests (e.g., test_user_login_fails_with_invalid_password) so failures are self-documenting.
Keep your test suite fast; if it takes more than 5 seconds to run, you will stop using it.
Treat your test code with the same respect as your production code—refactor it as your app grows.
Mock external API calls in your tests to ensure your test suite remains deterministic and fast.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing, start documenting your API endpoints and look into setting up a basic CI/CD pipeline to move beyond local automation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to code without fear of breaking your project. Use these tools to build a portfolio that stands out to recruiters and peers alike. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
